text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner and easier to understand. For example, you might rename unclear variables, break a long function into smaller parts, or remove duplicate code. By doing this, you increase readability, which helps both you and other developers work more efficiently. Refactoring also reduces technical debt, which is the cost of fixing messy code later. If you ignore technical debt, the code becomes harder to maintain over time. Regular refactoring keeps your codebase healthy and saves time in the long run.
